commit.message:
fix: guarantee prebuffer EOF sentinel and abort on prebuffer error

1. EOF sentinel (None) was silently dropped when the prebuffer queue
   was full — put_nowait(None) suppressed by QueueFull. Now makes room
   by discarding one data chunk before placing the sentinel, preventing
   _yield_from_prebuffer() from blocking forever.

2. After draining a failed prebuffer, get_audio_stream fell through to
   _signal_track_completion() — treating a CDN/HTTP error as natural
   track end, desyncing Ynison queue state. Now checks prebuffer.error
   after the yield loop and aborts instead of signaling completion.

commit.message:
feat: scale terminal to fit window while preserving aspect ratio (#32)

* feat: scale terminal to fit window while preserving aspect ratio

Measures natural dimensions at base 16px font-size on mount, then
computes a scale factor from the available viewport (minus 32px
padding on each side) and applies it as a dynamic font-size. All
sizing (80ch widths, 1.4em row heights, input field widths in ch)
inherits from the container font-size so everything scales uniformly.
Padding and status-line margins converted to em units so they scale
proportionally too. Resize listener keeps the terminal fitted when
the browser window changes size.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01U4Nxec54tKj4RuirBsTmkS

commit.message:
feat(js,python): add 16-byte binary encoding for SparkId strings (#7)

* feat(js,python): add binary encoding (toBytes/fromBytes, to_bytes/from_bytes)

Add 16-byte binary encoding for SparkId strings using 6-bit index packing.
Each Base58 character maps to a 6-bit index (0-57), packed MSB-first into
16 bytes (126 data bits + 2 padding bits). Conversion uses only table
lookups and bit shifts — no division or multiplication.

Properties:
- Sort-preserving: byte comparison matches string comparison
- Lossless round-trip: from_bytes(to_bytes(id)) == id
- 24% smaller than string representation (16 vs 21 bytes)

JS: toBytes(id) -> Uint8Array, fromBytes(bytes) -> string
Python: to_bytes(id_string) -> bytes, from_bytes(data) -> str

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(js,python): extract binary encoding and shared constants into separate files

JS:
- src/constants.ts: ALPHABET, BASE, ID_LENGTH, segment counts
- src/binary.ts: toBytes(), fromBytes(), DECODE table, packing constants
- src/index.ts: imports from constants, re-exports from binary

Python:
- src/sparkid/_constants.py: ALPHABET, BASE, ID_LENGTH, segment counts
- src/sparkid/_binary.py: to_bytes(), from_bytes(), _DECODE table, packing constants
- src/sparkid/_generator.py: imports from _constants
- src/sparkid/__init__.py: imports binary functions from _binary

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(js): remove .ts extensions from src imports for TS compatibility

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(js): tree-shakeable binary module with separate entry point

- Remove re-export of toBytes/fromBytes from index.ts
- Add src/binary.ts as separate tsup entry point
- Add './binary' subpath export in package.json
- Expand DECODE tables to 256 entries, remove ASCII_TABLE_SIZE constant

Users import binary functions via 'sparkid/binary' instead of 'sparkid',
so importing generateId alone pulls no binary encoding code.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* cleanup(python): remove redundant underscore aliases for shared constants

Use ALPHABET, BASE, ID_LENGTH, TIMESTAMP_CHAR_COUNT, COUNTER_CHAR_COUNT,
and RANDOM_CHAR_COUNT directly from _constants instead of re-aliasing them
with underscore prefixes in _generator.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(python): fix ruff line-length violations in _binary.py

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Optimize binary encoding and add benchmarks

JS (toBytes 3.4x, fromBytes 8x faster):
- Merge validation into packing loop (eliminate separate pass)
- Unroll all loops into direct assignments
- Add ENCODE Uint8Array and VALID_INDEX lookup table
- Shrink DECODE table from 256 to 128 entries
- Separate error messages for invalid index vs non-zero padding

Python (to_bytes 2.5x, from_bytes 1.7x faster):
- Merge validation into packing
- Unroll all loops into direct assignments
- Add VALID_INDEX lookup table
- Use "".join(tuple) instead of 21 string concatenations
- Shrink DECODE table from 256 to 128 entries
- Separate error messages for invalid index vs non-zero padding

Add --binary flag for benchmarks:
- JS: npm run bench:binary
- Python: uv run python bench/benchmark.py --binary

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(js): clean up binary.ts comment and formatting

- Fix comment: DECODE guard rejects chars outside 0-127, not 0-255
- Compact fromCharCode args formatting (no logic change)
- Restore error path to use direct lookup (not nullish coalescing)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: combine VALID_INDEX and ENCODE tables in fromBytes/from_bytes

Extend ENCODE to 64 entries with sentinel values for invalid indices
(58-63): 0 in JS, empty string in Python. This allows a single table
lookup to serve both validation and character encoding, eliminating the
separate VALID_INDEX table and saving 21 table reads per call.

JS: subtract-1-OR trick detects zero entries via sign bit propagation.
Python: empty strings are falsy, so the existing 'and' chain works.

JS fromBytes: ~0.09µs (unchanged)
Python from_bytes: 2.3→2.2µs (~5% faster)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf(python): use len check instead of 21-way and chain in from_bytes

Invalid _ENCODE entries are empty strings, so the joined result is
shorter than 21 chars when any index is out of range. A single len()
check replaces 21 truthiness evaluations.

from_bytes: 2.2→2.1µs (~5% faster)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf(js): use Math.min for fromBytes validation

Replace 21 subtractions + 20 OR ops with a single Math.min call.
V8 optimizes Math.min with integer args as a native intrinsic.

fromBytes: 0.091→0.079µs (~15% faster)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: move padding check before unpacking in fromBytes/from_bytes

Check non-zero padding bits early, right after length validation,
before doing any index extraction or ENCODE lookups. Fail-fast on
malformed input. No perf change on happy path.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add filesystem-safety validation for agent and team names

Agent and team names are used to derive worktree paths (ADR-034) but
were not validated for path-safety. The existing check enforced
non-empty, length, and uniqueness, yet the doc-comment claimed
filesystem-safe without rejecting path separators, null bytes, control
characters, or the reserved dot names '.' and '..'.

Add is_filesystem_safe() and call it from both validate_agent_names()
and validate_team_members(). Add unit tests covering path separators,
backslashes, control characters, dot/dot-dot names, and a team-name
variant. Existing tests are unaffected.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: harden api module against integer overflow from untrusted input

Audit of src/api/ found four integer-overflow paths reachable from
untrusted server data:

- stream.rs process(): buffer-length check used plain addition that
  could wrap on usize overflow; switched to saturating_add.
- stream.rs apply_chat_compat_tool_delta(): tool_call.index arrives
  from deserialized JSON with no upper bound; clamped to 1024 and
  used saturating_add for block_index.
- stream.rs ensure_chat_compat_tool_state(): resize target used
  index + 1 which could wrap to zero, truncating the vector; switched
  to saturating_add.
- client/mod.rs resolve_max_tokens(): float cast (n as f64 * 0.75)
  as u32 lost precision for large context sizes; replaced with exact
  integer arithmetic (n as u64 * 3) / 4.

No unsafe blocks, raw pointers, FFI boundaries, transmute, or
function-pointer casts exist in the api module. The remaining audit
categories (bad character handling, null-terminator assumptions,
stack allocation sizing, control-flow hardening) are clean by design.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: harden resolve_max_tokens and chat_compat_messages against edge-case panics

resolve_max_tokens: guard clamp(128, ceiling) against panic when a local
server reports n_ctx < 171 (ceiling < 128 violates clamp min <= max
invariant). Return the reduced ceiling directly in that case.

chat_compat_messages: replace unchecked messages.len() + 1 with
saturating_add to prevent theoretical overflow on extreme input sizes.

Add two regression tests covering n_ctx=100 and n_ctx=1 boundary cases.
